01_initial_request.input_data: AlphaFold2 predicted models and experimental cryo-EM density maps
01_initial_request.user_intent: To study the robustness of model refinement at lower resolutions and extend refinement success below 4.5 Å resolution using AlphaFold2 models

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Experimental cryo-EM maps exist at high resolution (2-4.5 Å), but lower resolution maps are needed to test refinement robustness. [Gap] Traditional simulated maps from atomic structures do not mimic experimental noise and features. [Decision] To achieve realistic lower resolution data, chose to use Situs (pdb2vol/vol2pdb) to perform Gaussian convolution on experimental maps, expecting to obtain hybrid density maps at 5, 6, 8, 10, and 12 Å resolutions.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Hybrid experimental-simulated density maps created retaining features of original experimental EM density.

references.relevance: Describes conventions and workflows for using Situs for resolution lowering and map simulation.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Amino acid sequences are known for the target proteins. [Gap] Atomic models are needed as starting points for refinement. [Decision] To achieve accurate initial models, chose to use AlphaFold2 to perform structure prediction on sequences, expecting to obtain predicted protein models with pLDDT confidence scores.

parameters.access_method: Google Colab Notebook 'AlphaFold with a density map' or local installation
parameters.selection_criteria: best confidence based on average pLDDT

02_agent_trajectory.observation: AlphaFold2 models obtained for 13 cases, 11 showing higher than 0.7 TM-score initially.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Raw density maps require preprocessing before refinement. [Gap] Maps may need sharpening to enhance features. [Decision] To achieve prepared maps for refinement, chose to use Phenix (phenix.local_aniso_sharpen) to perform anisotropic sharpening, expecting to obtain sharpened density maps.

references.relevance: Describes Phenix refinement protocols integrated with AlphaFold2.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Sharpened maps cover the entire unit cell or large volume. [Gap] Refinement should focus on the chain of interest. [Decision] To achieve focused refinement regions, chose to use Phenix (phenix.map_box) to perform cropping, expecting to obtain rectangular cropped regions containing the chain of interest.

parameters.output_description: sharpened, rectangular cropped region containing the chain of interest

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Box-cropped density maps prepared for refinement.

references.relevance: Describes Phenix refinement protocols integrated with AlphaFold2.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Initial AlphaFold2 models have minor inaccuracies. [Gap] Models need to be fitted to high-resolution experimental density. [Decision] To achieve improved accuracy, chose to use Phenix (refinement function) to perform model refinement against experimental cryo-EM maps, expecting to obtain refined models with higher TM scores.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Refinement successful for all 13 cases; 8 models achieved over 0.8 TM-score initially, enhanced to near or over 0.9 TM scores.

references.relevance: Demonstrated AlphaFold2 models can be refined against high-resolution cryo-EM density maps.
Describes Real-space refinement in PHENIX for cryo-EM.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Refinement success below 4.5 Å resolution is uncertain. [Gap] Phenix is designed for high-resolution maps. [Decision] To achieve extension of refinement success below 4.5 Å, chose to use Phenix (refinement function) to perform model refinement against hybrid maps with adjusted parameters, expecting to obtain refined models at lower resolutions.

parameters.resolution_limit: 2-3 Å larger resolution parameter than the nominal map resolution

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Refinement improved accuracy for 9 cases at 5 Å, 6 cases at 6 Å; success extended below 4.5 Å resolution.

references.relevance: Previous study suggested 4.5 Å limit, this step tests beyond that.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Refined models need quantitative accuracy assessment. [Gap] Visual inspection is subjective. [Decision] To achieve objective accuracy measurement, chose to use TM-align to perform structural alignment against true structures, expecting to obtain TM scores.

parameters.comparison_target: protein structure downloaded from the PDB

02_agent_trajectory.observation: TM scores calculated for all models; 9 of 13 cryo-EM maps attained good accuracies above 0.8 TM scores.

references.relevance: Describes TM-align method for calculating structural similarity.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Refinement performance varies at lower resolutions. [Gap] Understanding secondary structure detectability could explain performance. [Decision] To achieve insight into refinement challenges, chose to use DeepSSETracer to perform secondary structure segmentation on hybrid maps, expecting to obtain detected helices and beta-sheets.

parameters.input: box-cropped hybrid map of 8 Å resolution

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Beta-sheet region and most helices detectable at 8 Å; minor disagreement observed between model and segmented regions.

references.relevance: Describes DeepSSETracer tool for segmentation of secondary structures in cryo-EM density maps.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: TM-align structural comparison against PDB structures

TM_score.interpretation: Target quantifiable goal for model accuracy; achieved for 9 of 13 cryo-EM maps after refinement.

resolution_limit.interpretation: Refinement success extended below 4.5 Å resolution to 5 Å hybrid maps.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The initial objective was achieved. AlphaFold2 models were successfully refined to attain TM scores above 0.8 for 9 of 13 cases, and refinement success was extended below 4.5 Å resolution using hybrid maps.
